data.title: ‘Trump effect’ — just 1 in 3 Australians say Trump good for crypto: Survey
data.description:
Donald Trump’s election as US president has sent crypto markets soaring on his promises to back the sector, but only around a third of Australians say he’s good for crypto, according to a recent survey. 

Australian crypto exchange Independent Reserve’s survey of 2,100 local adults released on Feb. 21 found that 31% saw Trump as good for crypto, while 8% said he’s bad for the industry. 

The majority of respondents — around 60% — were neutral on the topic.

The survey found that crypto investors were far more positive about Trump than their non-investing counterparts. 

Half of Australian crypto investors said he was positive for crypto, while 44% were neutral. Only 6% said he was bad for the space. 

Of the non-crypto investors, only around 20% said he was positive for crypto, while 10% considered him bad for the sector. 

Bitcoin is trading at $91,100 and has jumped over 40% since Trump was elected on Nov. 5. BTC hit a peak of $108,786 on Jan. 20 — the same day he re-entered the White House.

“There is widespread anticipation that his pro-crypto policies will foster innovation and broader adoption of digital assets,” said Independent Reserve CEO Adrian Przelozny.

The survey comes after Swyftx said on Feb. 19 that a YouGov poll of over 2,000 Australian voters found that 59% of current crypto investors are more likely to vote for a pro-crypto candidate this election — which could mean a pro-crypto voting bloc of around 2 million Australians. 

Australia’s federal election must be held by May 17, and recent polls show that there could be a close race between the current center-left government and the center-right opposition.

Independent Reserve found that Australian crypto adoption has peaked since it started its survey in 2019, with almost a third of respondents reporting they currently own or have owned crypto.

Nearly 20% of those surveyed said their bank had prevented them from buying crypto or had delayed a payment to an exchange.

“Locally, while the sector is experiencing robust growth, challenges in regulatory clarity and market volatility persist,” Przelozny said.

“The actions of traditional financial institutions, such as banks blocking or delaying crypto investment activities, highlight the need for clear and supportive regulation to ensure the sector’s legitimacy.”

Luke Howarth, the shadow assistant treasurer and shadow financial services minister for the main opposition party, said on LinkedIn earlier this month that the ruling Labor government “has left much-needed regulation in the bottom drawer.”

“If we are fortunate enough to form [a] government, the [center-right] Coalition will work quickly to put in place fit-for-purpose regulation which keeps Australia up with the rest of the world and provides much-needed regulatory certainty,” he added.

Meanwhile, the Labor government, led by Prime Minister Anthony Albanese, wrapped a consultation on a crypto framework at the end of 2023, but its unclear when it will draft legislation.

data.title: BNK Banking Sells Around AU$220 Million in Mortgages to Bendigo and Adelaide Bank
data.description:
BNK Banking sold around AU$220 million worth of prime residential floating rate mortgages within the Bendigo warehouse facility to Bendigo and Adelaide Bank , according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

BNK will continue to service the assets on Bendigo and Adelaide Bank's behalf and receive a servicing revenue stream, according to BNK Chief Executive Allan Savins.

The bank expects to see a net sale profit of around AU$2 million, and the deal will be reflected in BNK's second-half financial results.

data.title: Elixir Energy Enters Farm In Agreements for Mongolian Gas, Renewables Assets
data.description:
Elixir Energy entered into two agreements to farm out its Mongolian gas and renewables assets, according to a Friday Australian bourse filings.

It entered into a farm in agreement for its gas assets with Gobi Terra (UK), establishing an incorporated joint venture holding Elixir's Mongolian coal bed methane business.

Gobi Terra acquired a 51% interest in the the joint venture and will fully carry Elixir through all costs to a final investment decision on a gas development.

It also entered into a farm in agreement for its Mongolia-based renewable energy business with Gobi Terra (UK), setting up another incorporated joint venture.

data.title: South Korean shares fall as chipmakers drag; set to end week higher
data.description:
Key points:
  • KOSPI falls, foreigners net sellers
  • Korean won strengthens against dollar
  • South Korea benchmark bond yield rises

Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** South Korean shares fell on Friday, dragged down by heavyweight chipmakers, but were still set for their second weekly gain.

** The benchmark KOSPI was down 11.56 points, or 0.44%, at 2,642.50 as of 0123 GMT, extending losses for a second straight session.

** The KOSPI has risen 2% this week, after rising 2.7% in the previous week.

** South Korean officials have requested an exemption from U.S. President Donald Trump's reciprocal, steel and aluminium tariff plans during their visit this week to Washington.

** The Bank of Korea will likely cut its key interest rate by 25 basis points on Tuesday, after a surprise rate hold last month, according to a Reuters poll.

** South Korea's government will prepare this month new measures to manage household debt.

** Chipmaker Samsung Electronics fell 2.05% and peer SK Hynix lost 2.83%, ahead of U.S. chipmaker Nvidia's earnings release next week.

** Among other index heavyweights, automakers, e-commerce firms and steel producers rose, but biopharmaceutical manufacturers fell.

** Of the total 939 traded issues, 496 shares advanced, while 364 declined.

** Foreigners were net sellers of shares worth 279.3 billion won ($194.67 million).

** The won was quoted at 1,435.0 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.07% higher than its previous close at 1,436.0.

** In money and debt markets, March futures on three-year treasury bonds (KTBc1) fell 0.01 point to 106.62.

** The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) rose by 0.7 basis points to 2.634%, while the benchmark 10-year yield rose by 1.0 bps to 2.881%.

($1 = 1,434.7000 won)

data.title: L3Harris pushes ahead on Arkansas rocket motor factories
data.description:
L3Harris Technologies said on Thursday it has begun constructing four solid rocket motor production facilities at its Camden, New Jersey, site, as demand for missiles accelerates amid escalating geopolitical tensions.

The expansion, part of a $215.6 million agreement under the Pentagon's Defense Production Act program, aims to increase domestic manufacturing capacity through the facilities, which will include propellant processing buildings.

The Camden facilities include a 60,000-square-foot space - big enough to optimize the component assembly process, reducing the distances some motors travel during manufacturing by 80%, thus speeding production. Other buildings at the facility include a dedicated mixer building to combine chemicals needed for the motors, and propellant processing buildings to support increased motor production.

The defense contractor makes rocket motors through its Aerojet Rocketdyne unit - which it bought in a deal that closed in 2023.

The unit is a key producer of weapon components as the U.S. seeks to replace aging Minuteman III intercontinental ballistic missiles and build an inventory of tactical missiles.

L3Harris has since increased spending on the unit by 84%, investments that have helped improve facilities and processes after years of under investment and late deliveries, Aerojet's former president Ross Niebergall told Reuters last year.

Demand for arms and military equipment has ballooned as a result of the Russia-Ukraine war and ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, with militaries around the world aiming to replenish stockpiles, benefiting contractors such as L3Harris.

data.title: Genting Singapore's Earnings Seen Supported by New Amenities, Attractions — Market Talk
data.description: Genting Singapore's earnings growth could be supported by its new phase of expansion at Resorts World Sentosa, say Citi Research analysts in a note. RWS is expected to see significant improvement in its earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization from mid-2025 onwards, they write. There are new non-gaming amenities opening in July. The recently opened "Minion Land" is also getting very good traction and positive feedback from visitors, they add. Citi maintains the stock's buy rating and target price of S$1.14. Shares are at S$0.76.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Levi & Korsinsky Announces the Filing of a Securities Class Action on Behalf of Monolithic Power Systems Inc. (MPWR) Shareholders
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Monolithic Power Systems Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/monolithic-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131105&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Monolithic Power Systems Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between February 8, 2024 and November 8, 2024.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (a) Monolithic's voltage regulator modules and power management integrated circuits were suffering from significant performance and quality control issues; (b) the defects listed in (a), above, had, in turn, negatively impacted the performance of certain products offered by the Company's largest customer, Nvidia, in which such products were used; (c) Monolithic had failed to adequately address and resolve known issues affecting the performance of the power management solutions the Company supplied to Nvidia; (d) Monolithic's relationship with Nvidia had been irreparably damaged due to the significant performance and quality control.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Monolithic stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/monolithic-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131105&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. data.title: Solasia Pharma to Cut Capital, Clear Losses, Elect New Directors; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Solasia Pharma will reduce its capital stock and legal capital surplus to offset retained losses, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The firm plans to cut capital stock by 2.11 billion yen and legal capital surplus by 1.52 billion yen, reallocating the amounts to clear its 3.63 billion yen accumulated deficit as of Dec. 31, 2024.

The move will not affect net assets, issued shares, or shareholder holdings.

The proposal will be presented at the March 26 shareholder meeting, with final approval expected by May 9. The company also plans to elect five directors and a new auditor.

Shares of the company gained nearly 3% in late-morning trade Friday.

data.title: Alcidion Group Secures AU$37.5 Million Contract With UK Trust; Shares Jump Near 1.5-Year High
data.description:
Alcidion Group secured a 10-year contract worth around AU$37.5 million with North Cumbria Integrated Care NHS Foundation Trust in the UK for the use of its Miya Precision platform to provide an electronic patient record, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The cloud-hosted, web-based solution will be provided through a smartphone-based app.

The firm will recognize AU$8 million to AU$9 million of the contract in fiscal 2025, depending on progress in project milestones.

Another NHS Trust Northumbria Healthcare NHS Foundation Trust is currently using the Miya Precision patient administration system module, the filing added.

Alcidion Group's shares jumped past 29% in early trading on Friday, hitting their highest since October 2023.

data.title: More from BOJ's Ueda - Ready to respond to abnormal market moves
data.description:
Bank of Japan Governor Ueda:

  • Our basic stance is that market forces determine long-term interest rate levels.
  • We expect long-term interest rates to fluctuate to some extent depending on the market's view on the economic outlook.
  • If markets make abnormal moves, we stand ready to respond nimbly, such as through market operations to smooth market moves.
  • Won't comment on where long-term interest rates could eventually converge.
  • Can't say specifically when exactly BOJ could conduct emergency market operations to soothe yield moves.

data.title: Domain Holdings receives $2.7b takeover offer from US-based CoStar Group
data.description: Digital property portal operator Domain Holdings Australia has told shareholders not to take any action after receiving an unsolicited takeover offer from America’s CoStar Group. CoStar – a NASDAQ-listed provider of commercial and residential real estate information, analytics and online marketplaces to the US and European markets – has lodged a non-binding indicative proposal to acquire 100% of the issued capital of Domain at $4.20 per share. The American company has also revealed that its proposed price will be adjusted for any dividends declared or paid by Domain prior to completion of the proposed transaction, excluding the $0.02 announced on 13 February 2025. Significant shareholder As at 20 February 2025, CoStar had acquired 16.9% of Domain’s ordinary shares. Implementation of CoStar’s proposal is conditional on it entering into an agreed scheme implementation agreement (SIA) with Domain on customary terms. CoStar says its SIA would likely be conditional on approval from the Foreign Investment Review Board. The Domain board has commenced an assessment of CoStar’s proposal and advised its shareholders that they do not need to take any action in relation to CoStar’s proposal at this stage. Interim CEO named Domain announced on 23 October 2024 that current chief executive officer and managing director Jason Pellegrino would be stepping down after six years leading the business. The company has since named Greg Ellis as interim chief executive officer while it continues its search for a permanent appointment. Mr Ellis – who has been on the Domain board since November 2017 and will continue in this capacity during the secondment – steps into the position on 17 February 2025 and has committed to the role for a period of up to 12 months. Mr Pellegrino committed to stay with the company for up to six months through the FY25 half-year results period and transition to a new leader. His final day with the company will be 28 February.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2041 ET - Chinese shares are higher in early trade amid positive sentiment boosted by Alibaba's earnings. Investors are positive about long-term prospects of Chinese tech stocks for their applications with artificial intelligence. Semiconductor and tech stocks are leading the gains with Cambricon Technologies up 4.3%, Huaqin Technology rising 4.9% and Hundsun Technologies adding 2.7%. Chinese auto stocks and home appliance stocks are leading the losses with Seres Group and SAIC Motor each down 1.2%. Haier Smart Home is off 0.3%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index rises 0.1% to 3355.20, the Shenzhen Composite Index gains 0.4% and the ChiNext Price Index is up 0.7%. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

2028 ET - Beacon Lighting is nearing an inflection point in the rate of earnings growth, says Morgans, as momentum in its trade business continues and lower interest rates give retail sales a shot in the arm. Analyst Emily Porter says Beacon Lighting has around 5% of a A$2 billion trade market for lighting and ceiling fans in Australia. "Building up share from this low base can more than offset the effect of a subdued overall market," says Morgans, which has an add call on the stock. The bank also points to signs that the retail cycle is beginning to turn. "We expect this to gather force as the recent interest rate cut by the Reserve Bank of Australia stimulates a recovery in consumer confidence," Morgans says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2017 ET - Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index edges 0.1% lower to 3923.06, tracking Wall Street's losses overnight. Disappointing sales guidance for the coming year from bellwether U.S. retail has raised worries over the U.S. consumption outlook, Commerzbank Research analysts say in a research report. Among the worst performers on the benchmark index, Singtel falls 2.7% and Seatrium drops 2.4%.Wilmar International loses 1.9% after reporting significantly lower net profit in 2024 than the prior year. Meanwhile, Singapore Airlines rises 2.2% after posting record revenue in fiscal 3Q. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2015 ET - Chalice Mining's metallurgical breakthrough signals lower operating costs at its Gonneville deposit in Western Australia and reduced complexity, but it may not satisfy investors, suggests Barrenjoey. Chalice says it's now able to produce copper and nickel concentrates from low-grade composites, removing the need for a high-cost hydrometallurgical process. Analyst Richard Knights says Gonneville could now generate a 19% internal rate of return. "However, our project net present value is still comfortably less than Chalice's market cap (A$565 million), even after modelling a debt funded doubling of capacity after four years of operation." Chalice needs a step-change in palladium prices or an ability to feed higher grade material to the plant for longer to excite investors, Barrenjoey adds. It retains an underweight call on the stock. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2012 ET - Inari Amertron could face further margin compression in 2H FY2025 due to rising overhead costs and a ramp-up in new product introductions, Maybank IB analysts Anand Pathmakanthan and Arvind Jayaratnam say in a note. They highlight that Inari may struggle to achieve its high single-digit segmental growth target with its anchor radio frequency segment posting its lowest 1H revenue in four years. The analysts cut Inari's FY2025-FY2027 earnings forecast by 26%, 14%, and 8%, respectively, citing a softer outlook. Maybank lowers Inari's target price to MYR2.70 from MYR2.93 but maintains a hold rating, noting that downside risk could be limited after a 16% share price decline since December last year. Shares are 3.8% lower at MYR2.55. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2007 ET - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index edges 0.1% higher to 1578.86. The KLCI may stay under pressure after Wall Street's overnight weakness and amid ongoing foreign fund outflows and weak sentiment, Apex Securities says in a note. However, this trend may be temporary as Malaysian stocks remain attractively valued, which could spur bargain hunting, the brokerage says. Investors should consider defensive sectors like REITs and financials for stable dividend yields and resilience against volatility, it adds. Among gainers, YTL Corp. adds 3.0% and Petronas Dagangan is up 2.2%. Meanwhile, Press Metal Aluminium is 0.8% lower and 99 Speed Mart Retail is down 2.1%. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1945 ET - Sonic Healthcare's earnings guidance looks achievable, if not conservative, to Morgans after the pathology services company took another step toward turning a corner in the pandemic's aftermath. Sonic expects FY 2025 Ebitda of A$1.70 billion-A$1.75 billion when currency swings are stripped out, and Morgans believes that strong underlying revenue growth supports that outlook. "After years of trying to right-size the cost base to better reflect the post Covid-19 world, labor costs are 'just about there', with operating leverage returning and profitability improving," analyst Derek Jellinek says. The bank is also upbeat about the potential for acquisitions and contract gains through the medium term. Sonic is down 1.4%. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1926 ET - South Korea's benchmark Kospi falls 0.3% to 2646.77 in early trade, as shipbuilding, insurance and construction-equipment stocks retreat. Foreign investors are net sellers as Wall Street's overnight declines are weighing on sentiment. Shipbuilders HD Hyundai Heavy Industries and Hanwha Ocean fall 2.6% and 2.9%, respectively. Samsung Life Insurance slides 2.4% after soft 4Q earnings. Compact construction-equipment maker Doosan Bobcat loses 1.6%. Meanwhile, index heavyweight Samsung Electronics is down 1.2%. USD/KRW is 0.3% lower at 1,433.75 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield is up 1.5 bps at 2.888%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

1924 ET - Air New Zealand's 1H result was better than expected by Forsyth Barr and was accompanied by a NZ$100 million share buyback, but still came with enough baggage to keep the bank at neutral for now. Air New Zealand signaled its engine maintenance challenges have worsened. It now expects to have up to 11 planes at any time grounded for maintenance in 2H, up from eight in 1H. "Air New Zealand is at least two years away from a normalized engine maintenance schedule, which leaves investors in limbo as to what is an appropriate and sustainable (as is possible for an airline) level of profitability to use for relative valuation means," analyst Andy Bowley says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1922 ET - Japanese stocks are lower in early trade as concerns about borrowing costs and U.S. tariffs continue. Brokerages and heavy-machinery makers are leading the declines. Nomura Holdings is down 1.0% and Mitsubishi Heavy Industries is 1.3% lower. The newest 10-year Japanese government bond yield is up 1.5 basis points at 1.455%, the highest level since November 2009, after Japan's stronger-than-expected inflation data. USD/JPY is at 149.70, down from 150.14 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock-market close. Investors are focusing on any developments in U.S. trade and foreign policies. The Nikkei Stock Average is down 0.1% at 38625.96. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

1917 ET - Whilst Peter Warren Automotive's 1H pretax profit was within its guidance range, it was a low bar to reach after the Australian car dealer downgraded expectations in December, Jefferies says. Peter Warren Automotive reported an underlying pretax profit of A$7.1 million, down 79% on a year ago. Its outlook statement was also downbeat, citing challenging conditions for new car sales. "It seems like more of the same for Autos--oversupply, soft demand and margin pressure with management focusing on hard to reduce operating expenses/revenue," analyst John Campbell says. "It's hard for us to see any material profit turnaround whilst these conditions persist." Jefferies has a hold call on Peter Warren Automotive, which is down 11% today. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1856 ET - Block expects its 1Q guidance to be at the low point for both gross profit growth and adjusted operating income throughout the year, CFO Amrita Ahuja says on a call with analysts. The parent company of Cash App and Square is facing tougher foreign-exchange comparisons in the early part of 2025. One of the main objectives for the year is to grow Cash App Borrow, Ahuja says, noting that it plans to expand it to additional customers and offer higher limits by improving underwriting, expanding to more states and integrating borrow with direct deposit. Another focus is the recent launch of Afterpay on Cash App Card. "We see this product as another way for customers to manage their money and to drive increased spending through Cash App Card," Ahuja says. Shares fall 6.4%, to $77.70, after-hours. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

data.title: Chinese Shares Rise Amid Positive Sentiment — Market Talk
data.description: Chinese shares are higher in early trade amid positive sentiment boosted by Alibaba's earnings. Investors are positive about long-term prospects of Chinese tech stocks for their applications with artificial intelligence. Semiconductor and tech stocks are leading the gains with Cambricon Technologies up 4.3%, Huaqin Technology rising 4.9% and Hundsun Technologies adding 2.7%. Chinese auto stocks and home appliance stocks are leading the losses with Seres Group and SAIC Motor each down 1.2%. Haier Smart Home is off 0.3%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index rises 0.1% to 3355.20, the Shenzhen Composite Index gains 0.4% and the ChiNext Price Index is up 0.7%. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Petronas Dagangan May Face Pressure on Long-Term Demand — Market Talk
data.description: Petronas Dagangan may face risks to long-term demand from a potential expansion of Malaysia's RON95 fuel subsidy reforms, as well as from decarbonization efforts and the electric-vehicle transition trend, TA Securities analyst Hafriz Hezry says in a note. The impending subsidy cut on its own is expected to affect only the top 15% of earners in Malaysia, with likely minimal immediate impact on the Malaysian downstream petroleum product retailer's sales volume as these consumers are unlikely to significantly reduce consumption, he says. TA Securities downgrades Petronas Dagangan to sell from hold following share-price gains over the past three months. It maintains a MYR18.20 target price. Shares are 5.5% higher at MYR20.32. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Hong Kong Shares Rise 1.8%, Led by Tech Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rises 1.8% to 22976.08 in early trade, led by tech stocks. Market sentiment is likely boosted by Alibaba's earnings which showed strong quarterly profits driven by growth in its cloud unit and e-commerce, the Saxo APAC research team writes in a note. Among advancers, Alibaba Group shares rise 8.7%, Tencent gains 3.3% and JD.com is 3.0% higher. The Hang Seng Tech Index adds 2.7%. Meanwhile, Henderson Land Development falls 1.9%, Sands China loses 1.7% and CK Asset sheds 1.3%. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Japanese Shares Extend Losses on Hot Inflation Data
data.description:
The Nikkei 225 Index dropped 0.5% to below 38,500, while the broader Topix Index fell 0.3% to 2,726 on Friday, marking a third consecutive session of declines.

The pullback came as hotter-than-expected inflation figures reinforced a hawkish outlook for Bank of Japan monetary policy.

Data revealed that core inflation in Japan accelerated to 3.2% in January, up from 3% in December, surpassing forecasts of 3.1%.

Headline inflation also rose to 4% from 3.6%, hitting a two-year high.

The benchmark indexes were further pressured this week by US President Donald Trump’s tariff threats and hawkish comments from Federal Reserve officials.

On Friday, notable losses included Disco Corp (-2.3%), IHI Corp (-4.1%), Advantest (-2.8%), Kawasaki Heavy Industries (-3.7%), and Mitsubishi Heavy Industries (-3%).

data.title: Japan's Core Inflation Accelerates to 19-Month High of 3.2% in January
data.description:
Japan's core inflation accelerated to 3.2% in January, the highest since June 2023, data released by Statistics of Japan on Friday showed.

The reading, which doesn't count fresh food items, is higher than the 3% core inflation last December 2024 and may strengthen a hawkish outlook for the Bank of Japan's monetary policy.

The headline consumer price index, which include all items, was up 4% over the same month in the previous year after a 3.6% gain in December. The reading is highest since January 2023.

Food prices, excluding perishables, accelerated to an 11-month high of 5.1% in January from 4.4% last December, while rent prices, on the other hand, grew by 0.2%, the report said.

Further, electricity prices and gas costs remained elevated with the absence of energy subsidies since May 2024.

data.title: Huawei founder told Xi China's concerns about lack of chips have eased, state media says
data.description:
Huawei's founder told Xi Jinping at a meeting the Chinese president held with private sector entrepreneurs that concerns China had about a lack of homegrown chips or operating systems had eased, Chinese state media reported.

The People's Daily on Friday published an article summarising what major Chinese company founders including Huawei's Ren Zhengfei, electric vehicle giant BYD's Wang Chuanfu and electronics maker turned automaker Xiaomi's Lei Jun spoke to Xi about on Monday.

Public discussion of the meeting, where six founders were given time to address Xi while the likes of DeepSeek's Liang Wenfeng and Alibaba's Jack Ma looked on, has been highly controlled. Previously, state media only released Xi's remarks and not details of what the founders said.

Ren told Xi that the worries China had about a "lack of core and soul" had eased and said "I firmly believe a greater China will rise faster," the People's Daily reported.

The phrase "lack of core and soul" dates back to 1999 when a former China technology minister made that comment about the country's information industry, with "core" referring to semiconductors and "soul" referring to operating systems.

Huawei has been one of the key forces behind Xi's goal for the country to become more self-sufficient, overcoming U.S. sanctions to produce its own advanced chips and an operating system.

During the meeting, BYD's Wang spoke about how the country's EV industry had started from scratch and "showed an impressive report card" while Xiaomi's Lei told Xi that even if the international situation changed, with Xi's leadership there was "nothing that cannot be overcome", the People's Daily also said.

The other three entrepreneurs who spoke were from chip firm Will Semiconductor , humanoid robot maker Unitree and China's largest feed maker New Hope Group, according to the People's Daily.

data.title: BIOA SHAREHOLDER ALERT: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP Reminds Investors of Securities Fraud Class Action Lawsuit Filed Against BioAge Labs, Inc. (BIOA)
data.description:
RADNOR, Pa., Feb. 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — The law firm of Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP (www.ktmc.com) informs investors that a securities class action lawsuit has been filed in the United States District Court for the Northern District of California against BioAge Labs, Inc. (“BioAge”) (NASDAQ: BIOA) on behalf of those who purchased stock pursuant and/or traceable to BioAge's registration statement for its initial public offering (“IPO”) held on or about September 26, 2024 (the “Class Period”). The lead plaintiff deadline is March 10, 2025.

CONTACT KESSLER TOPAZ MELTZER & CHECK, LLP:

If you suffered BioAge losses, you may CLICK HERE or copy and paste the following link in your browser: https://www.ktmc.com/new-cases/bioage-labs-inc?utm_source=PR&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=bioa&mktm=r

You can also contact attorney Jonathan Naji, Esq. by calling (484) 270-1453 or by email at info@ktmc.com.

DEFENDANTS’ ALLEGED MISCONDUCT:

The complaint alleges that, in the registration statement and prospectus issued in connection with BioAge’s IPO, Defendants made false and/or misleading statements and/or failed to disclose information pertinent to investors concerning BioAge’s STRIDES Phase 2 trial for azelaprag. Specifically, Defendants misrepresented and/or failed to disclose: (1) the potential for liver transaminitis in any of its previous clinical STRIDES Phase 1 trials and various preclinical tox studies; (2) that there were no safety concerns and that BioAge expected top line results and to meet its primary endpoint goals in connection with its STRIDES clinical trial; and (3) as a result, Defendants’ statements about the company’s business, operations, and prospects were materially false and misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis at all relevant times.

THE LEAD PLAINTIFF PROCESS:

BioAge investors may, no later than March 10, 2025, seek to be appointed as a lead plaintiff representative of the class through Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP or other counsel, or may choose to do nothing and remain an absent class member. A lead plaintiff is a representative party who acts on behalf of all class members in directing the litigation.  The lead plaintiff is usually the investor or small group of investors who have the largest financial interest and who are also adequate and typical of the proposed class of investors. The lead plaintiff selects counsel to represent the lead plaintiff and the class and these attorneys, if approved by the court, are lead or class counsel. Your ability to share in any recovery is not affected by the decision of whether or not to serve as a lead plaintiff.

Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP encourages BioAge investors who have suffered significant losses to contact the firm directly to acquire more information.

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR THE CASE OR GO TO: https://www.ktmc.com/new-cases/bioage-labs-inc?utm_source=PR&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=bioa&mktm=r

ABOUT KESSLER TOPAZ MELTZER & CHECK, LLP:     

Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP prosecutes class actions in state and federal courts throughout the country and around the world. The firm has developed a global reputation for excellence and has recovered billions of dollars for victims of fraud and other corporate misconduct. All of our work is driven by a common goal: to protect investors, consumers, employees and others from fraud, abuse, misconduct and negligence by businesses and fiduciaries. The complaint in this action was not filed by Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP. For more information about Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP please visit www.ktmc.com.

CONTACT:

280 King of Prussia Road

Radnor, PA 19087

info@ktmc.com

May be considered attorney advertising in certain jurisdictions. Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.

data.title: Yancoal Australia Coal Resources Fall Over 9% in 2024; Shares Up 7%
data.description:
Yancoal Australia reported measured, indicated, and inferred coal resources of 4.4 billion tonnes as of Dec. 31, 2024, falling 9.4% from the resources of 4.86 billion tonnes it reported in the previous year, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Its recoverable proved and probable coal reserves clocked in at 920 million tonnes during the period, falling 5.2% year-over-year from 970 million tonnes.

Its marketable proved and probable coal reserves fell 5.6% year-over-year to 680 million tonnes from 720 million tonnes.

The company's shares rose past 7% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Japan Rate Hike Hopes Get Boost From Fresh Uptick in Inflation — Update
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

​TOKYO--Japan's consumer inflation grew at the fastest pace in two years in January, likely adding fuel to speculation that an interest-rate hike is coming soon.

Overall consumer prices rose 4.0% in January from a year earlier, compared with the ​3.6% growth seen in December, government data showed Friday. It was the first time since January 2023 that the overall gauge reached 4%.

Higher energy and food costs contributed to the pick-up. Energy prices rose 10.8% in January, while food prices excluding fresh food increased 5.1%.

The index excluding volatile fresh food and energy prices, a measure of underlying inflation, rose ​2.5%, ​compared with the 2.4% rise in the previous month.

​The continued rise in consumer prices likely supports expectations for an additional rate hike by the Japanese central bank. Many economists expect the bank to raise rates around the summer, but some say the bank could act sooner, after recent data pointed to the strength in Japan's economy.

The central bank raised the policy rate to 0.5% in late January from 0.25%, and BOJ Gov. Kazuo Ueda has pledged to keep up the increases as long as the economy and inflation develop in line with the bank's projections.

Given that consumer inflation is accelerating again due to a rise in prices of goods such as food, market expectations of an earlier interest-rate hike are likely to heighten, said Takayuki Miyajima, an economist at Sony Financial Group.

That view sent Japanese government bond yields to multi-year highs this morning. The benchmark 10-year JGB yield reached 1.455%, the highest since November 2009. The policy-sensitive two- and five-year yields rose to 0.830% and 1.095%, respectively, the highest since October 2008.

"The Bank of Japan is in a very difficult situation," Miyajima said, however. "The growth in service prices, an important indicator of underlying inflation, shows little sign of acceleration--which would make the BOJ to be somewhat cautious about rate hikes."

Friday's data showed that service prices rose 1.4% in January from a year earlier, slower than the 1.6% increase seen in December.

Some economists say wage growth could add to services inflation.

"As strong wage increases are expected to continue, the pace of service price rises is likely to remain steady," said Mizuho Securities economist Ryosuke Katagi. Still, the acceleration marked in January isn't enough for the BOJ to achieve its goal of stable 2% inflation, he added.

Japanese companies are expected to give big pay raises again at annual wage talks, the preliminary results of which are due in March.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Medley to Merge Four Subsidiaries, Record Extraordinary Loss
data.description:
Medley will merge four subsidiaries, including AxisRoot Holdings and Pacific Medical, through absorption-type mergers, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The restructuring will result in an extraordinary loss due to the extinguishment of tie-in shares. The mergers, subject to shareholder approval, will take effect on Sept. 1.

AxisRoot Holdings became a Medley subsidiary on Jan. 31 and is set to become a wholly owned unit on April 30 via a share exchange.

Pacific Medical, already fully owned, has been integrated into Medley's operations through M&A.

Shares of the company declined over 1% in recent trade.
